title: ICE Killing of U.S. Citizen Ruben Ray Martinez in Texas Concealed for Nearly a Year
excerpt: FOIA records released by American Oversight revealed that ICE agents fatally shot 23-year-old U.S. citizen Ruben Ray Martinez in South Padre Island, Texas in March 2025—but the agency's involvement was not publicly disclosed for nearly 11 months. A witness's account contradicted ICE's claim that Martinez had 'intentionally' run over an agent.
content:
Records released February 2026 by watchdog group American Oversight, obtained through a FOIA lawsuit, revealed that an ICE Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) agent fatally shot Ruben Ray Martinez, a 23-year-old U.S. citizen from San Antonio, on March 15, 2025, in South Padre Island, Texas—the first known killing of a U.S. citizen by an ICE agent in Trump's second term.

  • Though Martinez's death was reported by local media at the time, neither federal nor state authorities disclosed that a federal immigration agent fired the fatal shots until nearly 11 months later.
  • ICE claimed Martinez 'accelerated forward' and 'intentionally ran over' an agent; the only passenger in the vehicle, Joshua Orta, stated in a written declaration that Martinez 'did not hit anyone,' that agents gave no warnings before firing, and that the car was 'barely moving.'
  • Body-camera footage subsequently obtained by CBS News appeared to show federal agents standing in front of the car as it slowly moved, contradicting the official account.
  • A Texas grand jury declined to indict the agent on February 25, 2026, over the objections of Martinez's family attorneys, who said they did not know whether the jury had been shown the contradicting video evidence.
  • ICE withheld information about its involvement for nearly a year, prompting Democratic lawmakers to call for an investigation into what Rep. Joaquin Castro called an 'eight-month cover-up.'
'These records demonstrate a stark disconnect between the constitutional standards on which ICE claims to train its officers and the abusive and deadly enforcement practices.' — American Oversight
mediaPlaceholder: South Padre Island Police Department bodycam footage showing Martinez's car slowly moving as agents fire
